prompt:
STYLE LOCK: Period gothic live-action, mid-19th-century interiors; candlelight and deep shadows; desaturated cool-gray palette; naturalistic skin and fabric texture; no illustration or stylized animation

CHARACTER VARIABLES (use these identities consistently; do not redesign faces or wardrobe):
- Character_OfficerBriggs <IMAGE_1> [Officer Briggs]: Also present (not shot focus); keep identity consistent: Man in late 20s to about 30, shorter stocky build, rounde.... Match reference <IMAGE_1>.
- Character_OfficerHale <IMAGE_2> [Officer Hale]: Also present (not shot focus); keep identity consistent: Man in late 30s, lean tall build, long narrow face, dark .... Match reference <IMAGE_2>.
- Character_Officer_Reynolds <IMAGE_3> [Officer Reynolds]: Also present (not shot focus); keep identity consistent: Man in early 40s, solid medium build, square jaw, short n.... Match reference <IMAGE_3>.
- Character_The_Narrator <IMAGE_4> [The Narrator]: Lean man of middle years (about 40–50), pale sallow skin, hollow cheeks, dark disordered medium-length hair, bright intense dark eyes, thin tense mouth; plain dark wool waistcoat over white shirtsleeves, dark trousers; period clothing. Visual lock: Same lean pale face, dark disordered hair, and bright intense dark eyes in every scene; always plain dark waistcoat and shirtsleeves as default; never elderly, never white-haired, never the filmed blue eye. Voice: Male, middle years; medium-high tense pitch; precise, controlled pace that sharpens into fevered urgency; intimate confessional energy, same voice on-camera and in V.O. Match appearance of reference <IMAGE_4> exactly.

CAST COUNT: exactly 4 distinct on-screen character identity(ies) only — Character_OfficerBriggs, Character_OfficerHale, Character_Officer_Reynolds, Character_The_Narrator. Do not invent extra people, duplicate faces, or crowd extras not listed.

AUDIO: REQUIRED native Grok off-camera voiceover. Character_The_Narrator narrates exactly: "I smiled,-for what had I to fear? I bade the gentlemen welcome.". Start speaking immediately with "I" — do not skip, delay, or swallow the opening word. After the last word, hold a brief natural pause with a closed mouth (about half a second); do not freeze mid-syllable or trail into empty staring. Do not lip-sync on-screen cast to this VO. Ambient/Foley: cloths lift, cupboards open. VOICE LOCK Character_The_Narrator: Male, middle years; medium-high tense pitch; precise, controlled pace that sharpens into fevered urgency; intimate confessional energy, same voice on-camera and in V.O.

Follow the camera framing and location in this prompt exactly. Prioritize the PRIMARY subject and ONE clear action with visible motion; background characters may stay mostly still.

THIS CLIP:
End cleanly when the spoken line and primary action finish — do not hold a frozen pose or empty silence after dialogue.
INT. HALL AND SITTING ROOM - NIGHT. also on screen: Character_OfficerHale <IMAGE_2>, Character_OfficerBriggs <IMAGE_1>, Character_Officer_Reynolds <IMAGE_3>. THE NARRATOR leads them through-opens cupboards, lifts cloths, shows empty corners. OFFICER HALE and OFFICER BRIGGS search with quiet thoroughness. Nothing. OFF-CAMERA VOICEOVER Character_The_Narrator <IMAGE_4> says "I smiled,-for what had I to fear? I bade the gentlemen welcome.". Character_The_Narrator <IMAGE_4> still wears dark waistcoat, white shirtsleeves, tailored trousers, indoor shoes Camera directive: Medium shot tracking beside Narrator as he smiles and welcomes officers, revealing empty cupboards and corners in dim night light. Performance: Acting intensity 5/10: Tight forced polite smile masking inner tension while calmly welcoming Optics: f/2.8 moderate depth of field Color grading: Kodak Vision3 500T 5219, desaturated cool-blue shadows, muted tungsten highlights, empty night interiors / 480p, 24fps
